g.data-narration: Welcome to learning the Karnaugh Map method, a powerful graphical technique for simplifying Boolean expressions in digital design.
K-Maps simplify Boolean expressions visually, reducing gates for cheaper, faster, and more efficient digital circuits.
g.text: Boolean algebra simplification can be cumbersome for 3+ variables.
K-Maps transform minimization into pattern recognition:

g.data-narration: A K-Map is a grid representing a truth table where each cell shows a minterm, and adjacent cells differ by only one variable.
g.text: A diagrammatic representation of a truth table - a grid where each cell = unique minterm.
Key insight: Adjacent cells differ by only ONE variable → can be combined!

g.data-narration: K-Map size depends on variables: 2-variable has 4 cells, 3-variable has 8 cells, and 4-variable has 16 cells in a grid.
g.text: For 5+ variables, use two 4x4 maps or Quine-McCluskey method.

g.data-narration: Gray code sequences ensure only one bit changes between adjacent cells, making logical adjacency match physical adjacency.
g.text: Gray Code - The Key to Adjacency
Headers follow Gray code: only ONE bit changes between adjacent positions.

g.data-narration: Adjacent cells include side-by-side, top-bottom neighbors, and wrap-around edges - all can be grouped to eliminate a variable.
g.text: Three types of adjacency enable grouping:
Example: m0 wraps to m8 (vertical) and m2 (horizontal)

g.data-narration: Step 1: Plot the function by placing 1s in cells corresponding to minterms, leaving others as 0s or don't cares.
Group 1s in power-of-2 rectangles, make groups as large as possible, cover all 1s, and use wrap-around for larger groups.
For each group, identify variables that stay constant - 1 means variable, 0 means complement. Variables that change are eliminated.
g.text: For each group, find variables that remain CONSTANT:
Example: Group of 4 cells (m0,m2,m4,m6) in 3-var K-Map:

g.data-narration: The final simplified expression is the OR sum of all product terms found from each group - this is the minimized SOP form.
g.text: Final Expression = OR (sum) of all product terms:

g.data-narration: Lets minimize F(A,B,C) = Sigma m(0,2,3,4,6) - a 3-variable function with 5 minterms.
We place 1s in cells for minterms 0,2,3,4,6. Notice m1, m5, m7 are empty (0s).
We form two groups: Blue group (4 corners, wraps around) gives B, Red group gives A and B.
The simplified expression is F = B + AB. Using Boolean algebra, this further simplifies to B + A, final answer.
g.text: From Blue group: B remains constant (0), so term = B
From Red group: A=0, B=1, C changes - term = AB
Optional simplification: B + AB = B + A (by absorption)

g.data-narration: K-Maps provide visual, efficient minimization for up to 6 variables using adjacency, grouping rules, and wrap-around properties.
